title: Papaver quintuplinervium
taxonomy: Papaver quintuplinervium is classified within the family Papaveraceae, which includes the poppy and meadow foam plants. It belongs to the genus Papaver and is a type of flowering plant. The species name "quintuplinervium" refers to its unique characteristic of having five main veins. This classification is based on its morphology and genetic characteristics. Papaver quintuplinervium is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia.

intro: Papaver quintuplinervium, also known as meadow poppy, is a perennial plant belonging to the poppy family (Papaveraceae) that is native to Central Asia and China. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antispasmodic effects that can help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. The bioactive constituents of Papaver quintuplinervium include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Papaver quintuplinervium include teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as dried and powdered extracts that can be used to support health and well-being.
